content_hints.description: The Dock Ward was long considered the most dangerous district in the city, though the Field Ward has since taken that title. This area is home to some of Waterdeep’s poor, and most of its taverns are inhabited by habitual drinkers.
content_hints.read_aloud: > You step into the Dock Ward, where the colors are burgundy and orange, and the air carries the smell of tar, frying fish, and the open sea. Timber buildings lean overhead, their upper stories jutting so far forward they nearly touch, blocking out the sky.

npcs.name: Dash
npcs.dialogue: You want information, I want silver. Everybody gets what they want, everybody walks away smiling. I seen things down by warehouse nine last night. Things with tentacles. That one's free -- consider it a sample.
npcs.notes: Street urchin, about twelve. Missing two front teeth. Runs a network of child informants across the Dock Ward. Genuinely knows everything that happens on these streets. Will not betray paying clients.

event_triggers: On entry: A pickpocket attempts to lift a coin purse (DC 14 Perception to notice).

content_hints.description: The Yawning Portal is a famous inn and tavern located in the Castle Ward of Waterdeep, featuring a common room with a 40-foot-diameter open well descending 140 feet to the first level of Undermountain. The place is a stone building with a slate roof and several chimneys.
content_hints.read_aloud: You see a painted sign of a dragon spitted on a lance creaking above the door. Inside, the ceiling hangs so low you duck on instinct, and smoke from a peat fire mingles with pipe haze, creating a permanent fog at eye level through which faces appear and vanish. The room is cramped, dim, and warm, and the smell of salt cod and cheap ale hangs in the air like a second set of curtains.

npcs.name: Staget
npcs.role: curb the violence in the Dock Ward

npcs.name: Lif
npcs.role: the former tavern is haunted by the poltergeist (specter) of the tavern’s previous barkeeper

event_triggers: On rest: Bonnie slides you an extra bread roll with your meal. 'You look like you need it. Do not get used to it.'

content_hints.description: The Dock Ward was long considered the most dangerous district in the city, though the Field Ward has since taken that title. This area is home to some of Waterdeep’s poor and is inhabited by habitual drinkers.
content_hints.read_aloud: You see the colors of the Dock Ward are burgundy and orange, and the folk of the Dock Ward take competition seriously. The air here is thick with the smell of weapon oil and something sweeter, perhaps cinnamon or clove, that has no business being in a Dock Ward warehouse.

npcs.name: Staget
npcs.role: curb the violence in the Dock Ward

npcs.name: Volo
npcs.role: last saw Floon outside the Skewered Dragon

event_triggers: On hostile entry: Yagra flips a table and draws twin scimitars. 'Wrong door, friend.'

content_hints.description: The Dock Ward was long considered the most dangerous district in the city, though the Field Ward has since taken that title. This area is home to some of Waterdeep’s poor and is inhabited by habitual drinkers in most of its taverns.
content_hints.read_aloud: You find yourself in the Dock Ward, a district long considered the most dangerous in the city, though some residents are glad of its reputation. You see the area is home to some of Waterdeep’s poor, with taverns inhabited by habitual drinkers.

npcs.name: Sergeant Cromley
npcs.dialogue: Another complaint? File it with the clerk. What? The clerk quit? Fine, file it with me. I will get to it. Eventually. We are understaffed, underpaid, and the captain says no overtime.
npcs.notes: Human, middle-aged, exhausted. Honest but overwhelmed. The Dock Ward has more crime than his unit can handle. Will cooperate with adventurers who do not cause more problems than they solve.

event_triggers: On quest available: Cromley has bounties posted for warehouse break-ins that he suspects are Xanathar Guild related.

content_hints.description: The Dock Ward was long considered the most dangerous district in the city, though the Field Ward has since taken that title. This area is home to some of Waterdeep’s poor, and most of its taverns are inhabited by habitual drinkers.
content_hints.read_aloud: You see wooden stalls crowd the wharf edge beneath canvas awnings snapping in the harbor wind. The colors of the Dock Ward are burgundy and orange, and the air is thick with the smell of brine and fish.

npcs.name: Old Maera
npcs.dialogue: Eel? Cod? Something from below the thermocline? I have a squid today with seven tentacles instead of eight -- lucky squid, they say. Or unlucky, depending on which end you are on.
npcs.notes: Human fisherwoman, weathered and sharp-eyed. Sells mundane and exotic sea creatures. Occasionally finds magical items in her nets. Knows the harbor currents, smuggling routes, and which warehouse doors are left unlocked at night.

loot.name: Cloak of the Manta Ray (tangled in fishing nets)

event_triggers: On browse: Old Maera leans close. 'I pulled something out of the harbor last night that was not a fish. Interested?'

content_hints.description: The Dock Ward was long considered the most dangerous district in the city, though the Field Ward has since taken that title. This area is where most of Waterdeep’s poor reside, and most of its taverns are inhabited by habitual drinkers.
content_hints.read_aloud: You see the Dock Ward, a district steeped in the colors of burgundy and orange, where the air carries the scent of the harbor. This area was long considered the most dangerous district in the city, though the Field Ward has since taken that title.
event_triggers: On exit: The sounds of the Dock Ward fade behind you as salt wind fills your lungs.
